body:
﻿5003-TC

Protected B when completed

2025

Form NS479

Nova Scotia Credits

You can claim these refundable tax credits even if you do not have to pay any
tax. If the total of these credits is more than the amount of taxes you have
to pay, you may get a refund for the difference.

Complete the calculations that apply to you and attach a copy of this form to
your paper return.

For more information about these credits, including credit eligibility
requirements, go to canada.ca/ns-tax-info.

Volunteer firefighters and ground search and rescue tax credit

Line 1 and line 62400: Volunteer firefighters and ground search and rescue
tax credit Claim $500 ^

Children's sports and arts tax credit

If applicable, complete the following table and include all of your
children's expenses for programs of artistic, cultural, recreational, or
developmental activity or physical activity. Assistance includes
reimbursements, allowances, and any form of assistance received or
receivable.

Do not include your receipts but keep them in case you are asked to provide
them later.

If you need more space, attach an additional sheet of paper.

*** Transcriber's Note: In print, the following table is set up as six
columns with the possibility of six entries. The columns are labelled as:
Column A: Child's name (one child per line); Column B: All eligible expenses
per child for the year; Column C: Assistance received or receivable for
amount in column B; Column D: Column B minus column C (Maximum $500 per
child); Column E: Amounts from column D claimed by others; Column F: Column D
minus column E. ***

Column A Child's name (one child per line) ^

Column B All eligible expenses per child for the year ^

Column C Assistance received or receivable for amount in column B ^

Column D Column B minus column C (Maximum $500 per child) ^

Column E Amounts from column D claimed by others ^

Column F Column D minus column E ^

Line 2 and line 62410: Your children's sports and arts expenses Total of all
amounts in column F of the table above = ^

Line 3: Applicable rate 8.79%

Line 4: Line 2 multiplied by the percentage from line 3 Children's sports and
arts tax credit = ^

Line 5: Line 1 plus line 4. Enter this amount on line 47900 of your return.
Nova Scotia Credits = ^

See the privacy notice on your return.
